description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG only) that draws a dense Transformer encoder-DECODER architecture diagram. Title 'Transformer (Encoder-Decoder)' at top in 32px bold. Two parallel vertical stacks side by side. LEFT 'Encoder' stack, bottom to top: 'Input Embedding', 'Positional Encoding', 'Multi-Head Self-Attention', 'Add & Norm (1)', 'Feed Forward', 'Add & Norm (2)' (these 4 attention/FFN sublayers wrapped in an 'N=6 x' container). RIGHT 'Decoder' stack, bottom to top: 'Output Embedding', 'Positional Encoding', 'Masked Multi-Head Attention', 'Add & Norm (1)', 'Encoder-Decoder Cross-Attention', 'Add & Norm (2)', 'Feed Forward', 'Add & Norm (3)' (the 6 attention/FFN sublayers wrapped in an 'N=6 x' container). Above the decoder, stack 'Linear' then 'Softmax' then 'Output Probabilities'. That is 20 labelled sublayer boxes total. Draw TWO cross-attention arrows from the top of the encoder stack rightward into the decoder's 'Encoder-Decoder Cross-Attention' box (one carrying Keys, one carrying Values, each labelled). Draw residual skip arcs on the OUTER side of each stack: 2 on the encoder's left, 3 on the decoder's right. Each box must be a labelled rectangle.
requirements: All 20 sublayer boxes (6 encoder + 8 decoder + Linear + Softmax + Output Probabilities + 2 N=6 containers visible) are visible within the 1920x1080 viewport with no clipping at any edge
No two sublayer boxes overlap each other
Every box label text is fully contained inside its rectangle with no overflow past the box edges
The encoder sublayers are vertically stacked with equal vertical gaps between adjacent boxes, and likewise for the decoder stack
The two 'N=6 x' containers each fully enclose their wrapped sublayers without any sublayer crossing the container border
The two labelled cross-attention arrows (Keys, Values) run from the encoder top into the decoder's 'Encoder-Decoder Cross-Attention' box and do not pass through any other sublayer box
The residual skip arcs (2 on encoder left, 3 on decoder right) do not pass through or overlap any sublayer box
The cross-attention arrow labels (Keys, Values) do not overlap each other or any box
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No horizontal or vertical scrollbar appears; all content fits inside 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) for a dense microservices system architecture diagram titled 'E-Commerce Platform Architecture' (32px bold, top). FOUR horizontal tiers, each a labelled band. Tier 1 'Client Layer': 4 boxes (Web App, Mobile App, Admin Portal, Partner API Client). Tier 2 'Edge Layer': 3 boxes (CDN, Load Balancer, API Gateway). Tier 3 'Services Layer': 8 boxes (Auth, User Profile, Catalog, Search, Cart, Order, Payment, Shipping). Tier 4 'Data Layer': 6 stores (Postgres, MySQL, Redis, Elasticsearch, Kafka, S3). Draw arrows: every client box to the Load Balancer; Load Balancer to API Gateway; API Gateway to ALL 8 services; AND these cross-tier/cross-service arrows that must route without passing through unrelated boxes: Order->Payment, Order->Shipping, Order->Cart, Search->Catalog, Auth->User Profile. Each service connects to at least one data store: Auth->Redis, User Profile->Postgres, Catalog->MySQL, Search->Elasticsearch, Cart->Redis, Order->Postgres, Payment->Postgres, Shipping->Kafka, plus Order->Kafka. That is 21 boxes and 25+ arrows.
requirements: All four tier labels (Client Layer, Edge Layer, Services Layer, Data Layer) are visible and not overlapping any box
All 4 client boxes, 3 edge boxes, 8 service boxes, and 6 data stores (21 elements) are visible within the viewport with no clipping
The 8 service boxes are evenly spaced horizontally within the services tier with equal gaps
Every box label is fully contained inside its box with no text overflow
No two boxes overlap each other, and tiers are vertically separated so boxes from different tiers do not overlap
Every one of the inter-service arrows (Order->Payment, Order->Shipping, Order->Cart, Search->Catalog, Auth->User Profile) is present and does not pass through any box that is not its endpoint
Each of the 8 services has at least one arrow to a data store, and these service-to-data arrows do not cross through unrelated service boxes
Arrowheads do not land on top of label text
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No scrollbar appears; the whole diagram fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing an algorithm flowchart titled 'Merge Sort (Top-Down)' (32px bold, top). Use standard shapes: rounded 'Start'/'End' terminators, rectangles for processes, diamonds for decisions, and a parallelogram for I/O. Flow: Start -> 'Read array A[0..n-1]' (I/O) -> 'call mergeSort(A, 0, n-1)' -> decision 'lo < hi?'. If NO -> 'return (base case)' -> back up to caller -> End. If YES -> 'mid = (lo+hi)/2' -> 'mergeSort(left half)' -> 'mergeSort(right half)' -> 'i=lo, j=mid+1, k=0' -> decision 'i<=mid AND j<=hi?'. If YES -> decision 'A[i] <= A[j]?'. If A[i]<=A[j] YES -> 'temp[k++]=A[i++]' -> loop back to 'i<=mid AND j<=hi?'. If NO -> 'temp[k++]=A[j++]' -> loop back to 'i<=mid AND j<=hi?'. If 'i<=mid AND j<=hi?' is NO -> 'copy remaining left then right' -> 'copy temp back to A[lo..hi]' (I/O) -> 'return' -> End. There are 14+ nodes, 3 decision diamonds, and at least 3 loop-back arrows. Every decision branch must be labelled 'Yes' or 'No' beside its outgoing arrow.
requirements: Start/End use rounded terminators, the 3 decisions use diamonds, I/O steps use parallelograms, and processes use rectangles
All 14+ nodes are visible within the viewport with no clipping at any edge
No two nodes overlap each other
Every node label is fully contained inside its shape with no text overflow
Each decision diamond has exactly two outgoing arrows, each labelled 'Yes' or 'No'
The Yes/No edge labels do not overlap any node or any other edge label
The 3 loop-back arrows return to the correct decision/node and do not pass through any unrelated node
Arrowheads do not land on node text and edges do not overlap each other where they can be routed apart
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No scrollbar appears; the whole flowchart fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing TWO stacked Transformer blocks in detail, titled 'Transformer Encoder Block (Internals)' (32px bold, top). Inside an outer 'N x 6' rounded container, stack these sublayers vertically from bottom to top: 'Input Embedding + Positional Encoding', 'LayerNorm (pre)', 'Q/K/V Projection (3 linears)', 'Scaled Dot-Product Attention', 'Concat Heads', 'Output Projection', 'Add (residual 1)', 'LayerNorm', 'Linear 512->2048', 'GeLU', 'Linear 2048->512', 'Add (residual 2)', 'Output'. That is 13 sublayer boxes inside the container. Draw two residual (skip) arrows on the LEFT side: one from before 'Q/K/V Projection' into 'Add (residual 1)', one from before 'Linear 512->2048' into 'Add (residual 2)'; these must route around the left without crossing any box. On the RIGHT, place a vertical stack of 3 small annotation boxes: 'h = 8 heads', 'd_model = 512', 'd_ff = 2048'. From the 'Scaled Dot-Product Attention' box, draw a small self-loop-style branch showing the softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d))V formula in a callout box on the right that connects back with a thin arrow.
requirements: All 13 stacked sublayer boxes are visible inside the outer 'N x 6' container with no clipping
The outer container fully encloses all 13 sublayers without any sublayer crossing its border
Every sublayer label is fully contained inside its box with no text overflow
Adjacent sublayer boxes are connected by vertical forward arrows and have equal vertical gaps
The two residual skip arrows run along the left side and do not pass through any sublayer box
The two residual arrows connect to the correct 'Add (residual 1)' and 'Add (residual 2)' boxes
The 3 right-side annotation boxes and the formula callout box are visible and do not overlap the outer container, each other, or any arrow
The thin arrow from 'Scaled Dot-Product Attention' to the formula callout does not pass through any other box
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No scrollbar appears; the whole diagram fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a finite state machine titled 'TCP Connection State Machine' (32px bold, top). States (circles, 12 total): CLOSED, LISTEN, SYN_SENT, SYN_RCVD, ESTABLISHED, FIN_WAIT_1, FIN_WAIT_2, CLOSING, TIME_WAIT, CLOSE_WAIT, LAST_ACK, CLOSED_2. Transitions with edge labels (18+): CLOSED->LISTEN 'passive open', CLOSED->SYN_SENT 'active open', LISTEN->SYN_RCVD 'recv SYN/send SYN+ACK', LISTEN->SYN_SENT 'send SYN', SYN_SENT->SYN_RCVD 'recv SYN/send ACK', SYN_SENT->ESTABLISHED 'recv SYN+ACK/send ACK', SYN_RCVD->ESTABLISHED 'recv ACK', ESTABLISHED->FIN_WAIT_1 'close/send FIN', FIN_WAIT_1->FIN_WAIT_2 'recv ACK', FIN_WAIT_1->CLOSING 'recv FIN/send ACK', FIN_WAIT_2->TIME_WAIT 'recv FIN/send ACK', CLOSING->TIME_WAIT 'recv ACK', TIME_WAIT->CLOSED_2 '2MSL timeout', ESTABLISHED->CLOSE_WAIT 'recv FIN/send ACK', CLOSE_WAIT->LAST_ACK 'close/send FIN', LAST_ACK->CLOSED_2 'recv ACK', SYN_RCVD->FIN_WAIT_1 'close/send FIN', SYN_SENT->CLOSED 'timeout' (a backward arrow that must route around without passing through other states). Mark CLOSED as the start state with an incoming arrow from nowhere. At least one self-loop: ESTABLISHED->ESTABLISHED 'recv data/send ACK'.
requirements: All 12 states are visible as circles within the viewport with no clipping
No two state circles overlap each other
Every state name is fully contained inside its circle with no text overflow
All 18+ labelled transitions are present with visible arrowheads at their targets
The ESTABLISHED self-loop is drawn as a loop returning to the same circle and is labelled
Each transition edge label is readable and does not overlap any state circle
Transition edge labels do not overlap one another
Edges do not pass through any state circle that is not their endpoint (including the backward SYN_SENT->CLOSED arrow)
The CLOSED start state is marked with an incoming arrow originating from no other state, and no element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No scrollbar appears; the whole state machine fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a deep decision tree titled 'Loan Approval Decision Tree' (32px bold, top), depth 4 with 14 leaves. No->leaf 'DENY' (red); 'Collateral >= Loan?' Yes->'Credit Score >= 650?' No->leaf 'REVIEW' (yellow); 'Co-signer?' Yes->'Co-signer Score >= 700?' No->leaf 'DENY' (red). Depth 4 leaves under the remaining depth-3 internal nodes, each Yes/No branch ending in a colored leaf among APPROVE (green), REVIEW (yellow), DENY (red): 'Loan < 100k?' -> APPROVE/REVIEW, 'Savings >= 6mo?' -> APPROVE/DENY, 'Debt Ratio < 0.4?' -> REVIEW/DENY, 'Credit Score >= 650?' -> APPROVE/REVIEW, 'Co-signer Score >= 700?' -> REVIEW/DENY. description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a dense UML sequence diagram titled 'OAuth 2.0 + Resource Access Flow' (32px bold, top). SIX participants with vertical dashed lifelines and a header box each: User, Browser, Client, AuthServer, TokenStore, ResourceServer. Time flows downward. Messages (horizontal arrows with labels), 18 in order: User->Browser 'open app', Browser->Client 'GET /login', Client->Browser 'redirect to authorize', Browser->AuthServer 'authorize request', AuthServer->Browser 'login prompt', User->Browser 'enter credentials', Browser->AuthServer 'POST credentials', AuthServer->AuthServer 'validate user' (a SELF-CALL drawn as a loop on its own lifeline), AuthServer->Browser 'redirect with auth code', Browser->Client 'auth code', Client->AuthServer 'exchange code (nested)', AuthServer->TokenStore 'store refresh token', TokenStore->AuthServer 'ok', AuthServer->Client 'access token + refresh token', Client->ResourceServer 'GET /data + token', ResourceServer->AuthServer 'introspect token', AuthServer->ResourceServer 'token valid', ResourceServer->Client 'protected data'. Show ACTIVATION BARS (narrow rectangles) on lifelines while a participant is processing. Use solid arrows for requests and dashed arrows for responses. The nested exchange (Client->AuthServer->TokenStore->back) should show stacked activation bars.

Below: 4 distribution switches 'Switch A (Floor 1)', 'Switch B (Floor 2)', 'Switch C (Floor 3)', 'Switch D (DMZ)' (each core router connects to all 4 switches). Each switch connects to 3 leaf endpoints: A->(PC-1, PC-2, Printer-1), B->(PC-3, PC-4, Printer-2), C->(PC-5, PC-6, VoIP-1), D->(Web Server, DB Server, Mail Server). Use a distinct icon-style shape per device class (cloud, shield for firewalls, rounded box for routers/switches, plain box for endpoints). Lines are undirected (no arrowheads).

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a dense cascade control-systems block diagram titled 'Cascade PID Feedback Control Loop' (32px bold, top).
